content.claim: Centralization of school governance in 2013, implemented as a non-structural policy reform, did not decrease inequalities in student achievement according to one quantitative study, suggesting that governance reorganization alone is insufficient to address tracking-driven inequality.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Single study line cited; results not replicated in notes
Centralization is a governance reform, not a tracking reform; cannot isolate whether non-structural reforms can substitute for structural change
Does not account for concurrent policy changes (curriculum, funding, teacher policy shifts)
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
